name: Horus (Healed)
shortDescription: Horus, the falcon-headed god, represents kingship and protection. This entity refers specifically to Horus after he was healed from injuries sustained during his battles with Set.
description: Horus, the falcon-headed god, is a central figure in Egyptian mythology, representing kingship, the sky, and protection. He is the son of Osiris and Isis. This entity refers to Horus specifically after being healed by Thoth, following injuries sustained during his battles with Set for the throne of Egypt. Set, Osiris's brother, murdered Osiris and challenged Horus's right to rule. The battles between Horus and Set were fierce and resulted in Horus losing an eye (the Eye of Horus). This injury caused him pain and diminished his power. Thoth, the god of wisdom and writing, intervened and healed Horus's eye. The healed eye became a powerful amulet symbolizing protection, healing, and restoration. The healed Horus represents the triumph of order over chaos, the restoration of justice, and the continuation of the pharaonic lineage. His healed state underscores the importance of resilience, healing, and the eventual victory of good over evil in the Egyptian worldview. He is often depicted with the Uraeus, the cobra symbol of royalty, further emphasizing his divine right to rule and his protective role.

sources.title: The Egyptian Book of the Dead
sources.author: Various Scribes

sources.title: Myths and Symbols of Ancient Egypt
sources.author: Lucia Gahlin
